scoring_methodology.clarity: 1-10: How clear and unambiguous are the instructions? Can a reviewer follow them without interpretation?
scoring_methodology.completeness: 1-10: Does the skill cover all topics its source guidelines require? Are there gaps in coverage?
scoring_methodology.actionability: 1-10: Does the skill produce concrete, structured outputs? Are pass/fail criteria defined?

strengths: Exceptionally detailed phase-by-phase evaluation with exact numerical thresholds
Quick reference tables and decision trees for rapid lookup
Clear output template with checklist format
Covers both current ICH Q1 and 2025 draft provisions
Severity classification table with concrete examples
weaknesses: Companion JSON file (stability-rules.json) has only 6 IDs out of ~100+ data points — not machine-addressable
No explicit cross-reference to skill-biotech-quality.md for biological products (could mislead if applied to biologicals)
scores.json_alignment: PARTIAL — SKILL.md is self-contained but JSON lacks IDs for automation

strengths: Covers the full analytical lifecycle (development → validation → verification)
Complete impurity threshold tables for Q3A/Q3B/Q3C/Q3D/Q3E with exact values
Decision tree coverage for Q6A specification setting
Q4B pharmacopoeial interchangeability restrictions clearly enumerated
Q7 API GMP audit checklist is comprehensive
Quick reference table of critical numerical thresholds
weaknesses: JSON file (analytical-impurity-rules.json) has ZERO IDs — entirely unaddressable for automation
No formal severity classification for findings
Output format lacks scoring/rating rubric
scores.json_alignment: POOR — JSON is well-structured content but has no IDs

strengths: Explicit rule ID citations throughout (VS-001, EC-001, etc.) — most traceable skill
Three decision trees for common regulatory questions (NGS, process change, viral clearance)
Common deficiency patterns section based on regulatory experience
Clear product scope definition (in-scope and out-of-scope)
Phase-based framework maps cleanly to CTD sections
Integration table linking to other ICH guidelines
weaknesses: JSON metadata claims 147 rules but actual count is 136 — metadata inconsistency
Q5C stability section could cross-reference skill-stability-analysis.md for general stability methodology
scores.json_alignment: EXCELLENT — All cited rule IDs exist in JSON; complete sequential coverage

strengths: 10-section evaluation framework covering Q8-Q13 comprehensively
Red flags section for each area provides rapid deficiency identification
Scoring rubric (0-3 per section) enables consistent evaluation
Quick reference table of critical distinctions (criticality vs risk, design space vs proven ranges)
Continuous manufacturing (Q13) coverage is thorough and current
weaknesses: Does not cite specific rule IDs from quality-systems-rules.json in the evaluation text
Output template references 'specific rule references from quality-systems-rules.json' but does not show examples
No cross-reference to skill-cmc-methods.md for analytical method lifecycle (Q14 overlap)
scores.json_alignment: MODERATE — JSON has rules but SKILL.md does not cite them by ID

strengths: Comprehensive 12-step review covering all S-series guidelines
Product-type routing table (Step 1) correctly identifies modified requirements per product type
Exposure margin framework with quantitative thresholds from S5(R3)
Covers gene therapy (S12), oligonucleotides (S13), and pediatric (S11) — forward-looking
Reference data table with all key numerical thresholds
weaknesses: Does not cite rule IDs from nonclinical-requirements.json despite that file having 70 well-formed IDs
Overlaps with skill-safety-pharmacology.md on S7A/S7B without cross-reference
No formal scoring rubric — output format uses PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL per study but no overall score
scores.json_alignment: MODERATE — JSON has excellent IDs (S1A-01 format) but SKILL.md doesn't reference them

strengths: Exceptionally detailed hERG assay best practice checklist (15+ sub-items)
Modality-specific routing table for scope determination
Decision trees for S7B package, TQT requirement, and follow-up triggers
Integrated risk assessment framework per E14/S7B Q&As
Timing alignment matrix for each clinical development stage
Risk classification with clear definitions (LOW/MODERATE/HIGH/CRITICAL)
weaknesses: JSON file (safety-pharmacology-rules.json) has ZERO IDs
No cross-reference to skill-nonclinical-review.md (which covers S7A/S7B at a higher level)
Metabolite section (6A) could be more detailed on when metabolite SP studies are warranted
scores.json_alignment: POOR — JSON has no IDs despite well-structured content

strengths: 583 rules with complete JSON alignment — largest and most comprehensive skill
Explicit rule ID citations throughout (E8-005, E9-004, etc.)
E9(R1) estimands framework covered in detail
ICH E20 adaptive design coverage is current and thorough
Weighted scoring methodology with category breakdown
Clear integration points (upstream dependencies, downstream consumers)
Usage examples for different scenarios
weaknesses: E9-R1-001 format in SKILL.md vs E9R1-001 in JSON — cosmetic mismatch could cause lookup failures
Length may be overwhelming for a single evaluation session
No cross-reference to skill-safety-reporting.md for E14 cardiac safety overlap
scores.json_alignment: EXCELLENT — 583 rules, all IDs verified, complete coverage

strengths: Covers entire safety reporting lifecycle: definitions (E2A) → ICSR (E2B) → PBRER (E2C) → post-market (E2D) → PV planning (E2E) → DSUR (E2F) → cardiac (E14) → selective collection (E19) → adaptive (E20) → pregnancy (E21)
E2B(R3) data element table with exact field requirements
Null flavor usage rules clearly documented
PBRER 20-section structural checklist
DSUR 20-section structural checklist with all 12 line listing fields
Timeline compliance summary table
Cross-cutting evaluation for MedDRA coding and controlled vocabulary
Output format is clear and structured
weaknesses: JSON file (safety-reporting-rules.json) has ZERO IDs — major gap for automation
No cross-reference to skill-eu-pharmacovigilance.md for EU-specific PSUR requirements
E22 (RWE) and E23 mentioned in frontmatter but not detailed in evaluation sections
scores.json_alignment: POOR — SKILL.md is excellent but JSON lacks IDs entirely

strengths: 8 domain-specific evaluation checklists (E5, E6, E11/E11A, E12, E15, E16, E17, E18)
Cross-cutting integration checks table bridges all domains
Pediatric extrapolation framework (E11A) is detailed and current
MRCT consistency assessment (E17) includes preservation-of-fraction method
Genomic sampling (E18) checklist is uniquely comprehensive
PASS criteria defined for each domain
weaknesses: JSON metadata claims 412 rules but only 132 exist — CRITICAL count discrepancy
SKILL.md states '412 rules across 7 domains + 7 cross-cutting rules' which propagates the JSON error
No formal severity classification for individual findings within domains
No cross-reference to skill-clinical-design.md for E7/E1 thresholds
scores.json_alignment: MODERATE — IDs exist and are complete for the 132 actual rules, but count claim is wrong by 3x

strengths: 249 rules with complete JSON alignment
5 critical cross-domain patterns clearly articulated
Decision matrix with 6-step routing
Domain interaction statistics quantify cross-domain relationships
Cross-reference map identifies most-cited guidelines
Implementation notes are practical and specific
weaknesses: No output template — the skill describes what to check but not how to format findings
No scoring rubric for cross-domain compliance assessment
M13C coverage is minimal (1 rule noted) despite being important for NTI drugs
No worked examples showing how the decision matrix produces an assessment

strengths: 4-level check framework (structural → cross-reference → jurisdiction → CRL risk)
CRL risk assessment based on 419 real Complete Response Letters — data-driven
Three-jurisdiction comparison (FDA/EMA/PMDA) with specific criteria
Bioequivalence acceptance criteria comparison table
Automation architecture section (Tier 1/2/3) shows implementation path
Key regulatory timelines table is a valuable quick reference
weaknesses: US FDA section of regulatory-requirements.json has ZERO IDs
Does not reference jurisdiction-divergences.json (DIV-001 format) in the SKILL.md text
Level 4 (CRL risk) is described but has no structured checklist
No cross-reference to v3 EU skills or Canada skill for deeper jurisdiction-specific analysis
scores.json_alignment: PARTIAL — divergences file is well-IDed; requirements file has sparse IDs

strengths: 7-step analysis framework covering entire EU regulatory lifecycle

Post-authorisation maintenance section (renewal, sunset clause, variations)

weaknesses: JSON (eu-legislation-requirements.json) claims 412 requirements but only 139 exist — 66% shortfall
No cross-reference to skill-eu-gmp-analysis.md or skill-eu-pharmacovigilance.md
Missing coverage of Regulation (EU) 2024/1938 (amended 726/2004) if applicable
Pharmacovigilance section overlaps with skill-eu-pharmacovigilance.md without delineation
scores.json_alignment: MODERATE — IDs exist and are well-formatted but count is critically inflated

top_recommendations.action: Fix factual error in skill-eu-gmp-analysis.md Gap #13 — BCS Class III biowaivers ARE accepted by EMA

Add rule IDs to 6 zero-ID JSON files: analytical-impurity-rules.json, safety-pharmacology-rules.json, safety-reporting-rules.json, health-canada-requirements.json, who-requirements.json, japan-specific-requirements.json
top_recommendations.impact: Enables machine-readable rule citation and automated compliance checking

top_recommendations.action: Correct metadata count discrepancies in special-populations-rules.json (claims 412, has 132) and eu-legislation-requirements.json (claims 412, has 139)

Add cross-references between overlapping skills: (a) nonclinical↔safety-pharmacology, (b) safety-reporting↔eu-pharmacovigilance, (c) all three EU skills to each other, (d) regulatory-compliance↔canada↔multi-jurisdiction
top_recommendations.impact: Prevents users from applying one skill in isolation and missing requirements covered by another

top_recommendations.action: Harmonize severity scales or add a mapping table showing how each skill's scale relates to a common framework
Add output templates to skill-cross-domain.md and skill-multi-jurisdiction.md (both currently lack structured output format)
top_recommendations.impact: Improves actionability of the two skills that scored lowest on this dimension
